name: pypiserver
vendor: pypiserver project (community-maintained; originally by Ralf Schmitt)
perf_notes: By default rescans the entire packages directory on every HTTP request, causing noticeable slowdowns at thousands of packages; optional directory caching via the watchdog-backed cached-dir backend (pip install pypiserver[cache], --backend auto/cached-dir) mitigates this. Runs single-process under bottle/wsgiref by default; concurrency comes from running it under gunicorn/cherrypy/twisted/waitress (--server). Uploads stream in 1MB chunks to bound memory. Docs report a real-world deployment serving 1000+ package downloads/min at peak when fronted by nginx response caching.
scale_notes: No database; serves from plain directories (local filesystem only). Reads are largely stateless, so replicas can run behind a load balancer over shared storage (e.g. NFS), but there is no built-in clustering, HA, or coordination. The cached-dir backend keeps a per-process in-memory index invalidated by filesystem events, so cache state is not shared across nodes and uploads must reach the node(s) holding the cache. TLS/HTTPS is expected to be terminated by a reverse proxy (nginx/Traefik/Apache).
summary: Minimal, dependency-light PyPI-compatible index server (built on Bottle) that serves and accepts uploads of packages from plain directories — the simplest way to self-host a private package index.
extras: Redirect-fallback to pypi.org (or a custom --fallback-url, default https://pypi.org/simple/) for packages not found locally: the client is 302/301-redirected to upstream rather than the content being proxied/cached; --disable-fallback turns this off (and is the dependency-confusion guard)

pypi-server update subcommand prints (or with -x executes) pip commands to refresh locally-seeded packages from PyPI; --ignorelist-file excludes private packages to avoid pulling malicious higher versions
Pluggable WSGI server backends via --server (paste, cherrypy, twisted, gunicorn, gevent, waitress, aiohttp, wsgiref, etc.) since it ships its own bottle copy
Pluggable ad-hoc authentication via an 'auther' callable in the Python API (documented PAM/python-pam example), making LDAP/custom auth possible without shipping it
Per-action auth granularity: -a chooses which of download/list/update require htpasswd auth (default: only update/upload)
Configurable link-hash algorithm (--hash-algo, any hashlib algo; sha256 default) or disable hashes entirely
Legacy /:project/json metadata endpoint (PyPI-style, also used by MicroPython upip), distinct from PEP 691 JSON simple — the index itself is HTML-only
XML-RPC /RPC2 'search' endpoint (name-only matching); the v3.0.0 line hardens its parser with defusedxml against billion-laughs DoS. pip search no longer works against /simple/
--server-base-url URL-prefix hosting plus X-Forwarded-Host support for reverse-proxy/ingress deployments
Accepts and serves PGP .asc signatures uploaded alongside packages (twine --sign); validates the .asc filename matches its package but does not verify signatures
Upload API supports a remove_pkg :action (delete-by-removal); there is no PEP 592 yank
Overwrite protection on by default (rejects re-upload of an existing filename unless --overwrite)
Multiple package roots are merged into a single index; paste composite apps can expose several indexes on different URL paths
Accepts documentation uploads but intentionally discards them (does not write to disk)

Configurable health endpoint (--health-endpoint, default /health, returns 'Ok'); customizable home/welcome page via --welcome; official multi-arch Docker images (latest=stable, unstable=main) plus community ARM64 image
Configurable logging format strings (--log-frmt/--log-req-frmt/--log-res-frmt/--log-err-frmt) and --log-file/--log-stream; default is pipe-delimited text, not JSON
pip install extras: [passlib] for htpasswd auth, [cache] for the watchdog directory cache
Configuration is via CLI flags only — no environment-variable configuration of server options (latest release v2.4.1, 2026-02-10; requires Python 3.10+ / PyPy3)
